sublevels.topic: Basic Structure & I/O â€” Beginner Level

questions.question: What is the entry point for every C++ program?
Which header must be included to use `std::cout`?
Which object prints output to the console?
What does `<<` do with `std::cout`?
options: Inserts data into the output stream
questions.answer: Inserts data into the output stream

questions.question: What does `return 0;` at the end of `main()` signal?
Which object reads input from the user?
What does `std::` before `cout` mean?
What is the purpose of `;` in C++?
Which operator is used with `std::cin` to read input?
How do you write a single-line comment in C++?

sublevels.topic: Basic Structure & I/O â€” Intermediate Level
sublevels.theory:
**`using namespace std;`** â€” eliminates the need to prefix standard library names with `std::`. Use with care in large projects (name collisions).

**`std::endl` vs `\n`** â€” both insert a newline, but `std::endl` also flushes the output buffer (slower).

**`std::cerr`** â€” standard error stream (unbuffered).
**`std::clog`** â€” standard log stream (buffered).

**Formatted output** with `<iomanip>`:
```cpp
#include <iomanip>
std::cout << std::setw(10) << std::left << "Name" << std::setw(5) << 42 << '\n';
```

**`std::getline(std::cin, str)`** â€” reads an entire line (including spaces) into a string.

questions.question: What does `using namespace std;` allow?
options: Omits the `std::` prefix for standard library names
questions.answer: Omits the `std::` prefix for standard library names

questions.question: What is the difference between `std::endl` and `'\n'`?
options: `std::endl` also flushes the output buffer; `'\n'` does not
questions.answer: `std::endl` also flushes the output buffer; `'\n'` does not

questions.question: What is `std::cerr` used for?
Which function reads an entire line including spaces?
Which header provides `std::setw` for formatted output?
What does `std::setw(10)` do?
options: Sets the field width for the next output item
questions.answer: Sets the field width for the next output item

questions.question: What does `std::cin.ignore()` do?
options: Discards a character from the input buffer (useful after >>)
questions.answer: Discards a character from the input buffer (useful after >>)

questions.question: What does `std::cin.fail()` return?
options: `true` if the last extraction failed (e.g., type mismatch)
questions.answer: `true` if the last extraction failed (e.g., type mismatch)

questions.question: What is the output of `std::cout << std::boolalpha << true;`?
Which stream is guaranteed to show output immediately without buffering?

sublevels.topic: Basic Structure & I/O â€” Expert Level

questions.question: What does `std::ios::sync_with_stdio(false)` accomplish?
options: Disables sync between C stdio and C++ streams for faster I/O
questions.answer: Disables sync between C stdio and C++ streams for faster I/O

questions.question: What is `std::cin.tie(nullptr)` commonly used for?
options: Disconnect cin from cout so cout isn't flushed before every cin read (faster I/O)
questions.answer: Disconnect cin from cout so cout isn't flushed before every cin read (faster I/O)

questions.question: Which C++20 feature provides type-safe, Pythonic string formatting?
What does `std::ostringstream` allow you to do?
options: Read from a string as if it were a stream
Build a string using `<<` operators in memory
questions.answer: Build a string using `<<` operators in memory

questions.question: How can you redirect `std::cout` output to a file at runtime?
What is `std::streambuf`?
options: A buffer for file reads only
The abstract base class for all I/O stream buffers
questions.answer: The abstract base class for all I/O stream buffers

questions.question: What does `std::cout << std::hex << 255;` output?
What is a potential danger of `using namespace std;` in header files?
options: Pollutes the namespace of every file that includes that header
questions.answer: Pollutes the namespace of every file that includes that header

questions.question: What does `std::cin >> std::ws` do before `getline`?
options: Skips leading whitespace (including leftover newlines in the buffer)
questions.answer: Skips leading whitespace (including leftover newlines in the buffer)

questions.question: Which C++23 function is a simpler replacement for `std::cout << std::format(...)`?
